one_time_description: Proceeds from sale of assets ($72,000) and transfer from deferred revenue ($520)
economic_notes: Three government business enterprises: Kumcheen Enterprises Ltd. (100%), Lytton Development Corp. (100%), Tlkemcheen Economic Development LLP (99.99%). Subsequent event: Lytton Creek wildfire on June 30, 2021 destroyed 5 band buildings and 39 houses on three reserves, plus the Band office. Significant rebuilding costs expected.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2021 (not 2024). Emphasis of Matter paragraph in audit report regarding subsequent Lytton Creek wildfire (June 30, 2021) which destroyed 5 band buildings, 39 houses, and the Band office — opinion not qualified. Social Development and Health are combined as one segment in statements ($3,958,970). Segments consolidated include Operating Fund, Social Housing Fund, Capital Fund, Trust Fund, and Enterprise Fund. Three GBEs on modified equity basis. Capital Fund had a large deficit for the year (-$728,357 surplus i.e. -$656,357 net after other items). No remuneration schedule found in the provided text. total_government_transfers estimated as ISC ($11,593,985) + CMHC ($514,563) + FNHA ($1,672,671) + Province of BC ($312,377) + Dept of Justice ($85,580) + Legal Aid ($20,411) + FNESC ($377,259) + BC Hydro ($70,259) + FNLM ($28,129) + Works program ($13,727) = $14,688,961 approximately; osr calculated as total revenue minus total government transfers.
